# Infermux

## Generated File

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `infermux-config.yaml` | YAML configuration with per-role model routing entries |

## How It Works

The `agentroles` command reads your `agentroles.yaml` and produces Infermux multi-provider router config:

```yaml

# Generated by agentroles — source of truth: agentroles.yaml

# Infermux multi-provider router config

version: 1
source: agentroles.yaml
model_routes:

  - role: planner

    provider: anthropic
    model: claude-opus-4-8
    route_name: role-planner
    fallback:

      - openai/gpt-5.5

    max_cost_per_call_usd: 0.50

  - role: implementer

    provider: anthropic
    model: claude-haiku-4-5
    route_name: role-implementer
    fallback:

      - openai/gpt-4o-mini

    max_cost_per_call_usd: 0.02
```

Each route entry includes:

- `role` — the role name
- `provider` — extracted from the primary model string
- `model` — extracted model ID
- `route_name` — `role-<rolename>` for identification
- `fallback` — ordered fallback model list
- `max_cost_per_call_usd` — optional cost ceiling

## Usage

```bash
agentroles build

# Start Infermux with --config infermux-config.yaml

```

## Limitations

- Provider/model extraction assumes `provider/model-id` format — adjust if Infermux expects different naming
- `max_cost_per_call_usd` is informational; Infermux may require additional setup for cost enforcement
- Route selection logic (headers, path, etc.) is not configured — add routing rules to Infermux separately
- Observability config generated only when `cost_tracking` is enabled in `agentroles.yaml`
